{{Short description|none}}
{{Short description|Men's national association football team representing Seychelles}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Seychelles women's national football team}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2024}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name                  = Seychelles
| Badge                 = 
| Nickname              = ''The Pirates''
| Association           = [[Seychelles Football Federation]] (SFF)
| Confederation         = [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] (Africa)
| Sub-confederation     = [[Council of Southern Africa Football Associations|COSAFA]] (Southern Africa)
| Coach                 = [[Ralph Jean-Louis]] 
| Captain               = [[Charmaine Häusl]]
| Most caps             = [[Benoit Marie]] (64)
| Top scorer            = [[Philip Zialor]] (14)
| Home Stadium          = [[Stade Linité]]
| FIFA Trigramme        = SEY
| FIFA Rank             = {{FIFA World Rankings|SEY}}
| FIFA max              = 129
| FIFA max date         = October 2006
| FIFA min              = 204
| FIFA min date         = April 2026 – June 2026
| Elo Rank              = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Seychelles}}
| Elo max               = 168
| Elo max date          = July 2008
| Elo min               = 199
| Elo min date          = January 2020
| pattern_la1           = _sey25h
| pattern_b1            = _sey25h
| pattern_ra1           = _sey25h
| pattern_sh1           = _adidaswhite
| leftarm1              = FF0000
| body1                 = FF0000
| rightarm1             = FF0000
| shorts1               = FF0000
| socks1                = FF0000
| pattern_la2           = _sey25a
| pattern_b2            = _sey25a
| pattern_ra2           = _sey25a
| pattern_sh2           = _adidaswhite
| pattern_so2           =_cruzeiro22a
| leftarm2              = 0000ff
| body2                 = 0000ff
| rightarm2             = 0000ff
| shorts2               = 0000ff
| socks2                = 0000ff
| First game            = {{flagicon|REU}} [[Réunion national football team|Réunion]] 2–0 {{fb-rt|SEY|1961}} <br> ([[Saint-Denis, Réunion]]; 13 February 1974)
| Largest win           = {{fb|SEY|1977}} 9–0 {{fb-rt|MDV}} <br> ([[Saint-Pierre, Réunion]]; 27 August 1979)
| Largest loss          = {{fb|CIV}} 9–0 {{fb-rt|SEY}} <br> ([[Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire]]; 17 November 2023) 
| World cup apps        = 
| World cup first       = 
| World cup best        = 
| Regional name         = [[Africa Cup of Nations]]
| Regional cup apps     = 
| Regional cup first    = 
| Regional cup best     = 
| 2ndRegional name      = [[COSAFA Cup]]
| 2ndRegional cup apps  = 14
| 2ndRegional cup first = [[2005 COSAFA Cup|2005]]
| 2ndRegional cup best  = Group stage ([[2005 COSAFA Cup|2005]], [[2006 COSAFA Cup|2006]], [[2007 COSAFA Cup|2007]], [[2008 COSAFA Cup|2008]], [[2009 COSAFA Cup|2009]], [[2013 COSAFA Cup|2013]], [[2015 COSAFA Cup|2015]], [[2016 COSAFA Cup|2016]], [[2017 COSAFA Cup|2017]], [[2018 COSAFA Cup|2018]], [[2019 COSAFA Cup|2019]], [[2022 COSAFA Cup|2022]], [[2023 COSAFA Cup|2023]], [[2024 COSAFA Cup|2024]])
|pattern_la3=_sey25t|pattern_b3=_sey25t|pattern_ra3=_sey25t|leftarm3=ffffff|shorts3=ffffff|rightarm3=ffffff|body3=ffffff|socks3=ffffff|pattern_so3=_ad|pattern_so1=_bayern2324h}}

The '''Seychelles national football team''', nicknamed ''the Pirates'', represents [[Seychelles]] in international [[association football|football]] and is controlled by the [[Seychelles Football Federation]] (SFF). SFF has been a member of the [[Confederation of African Football]] (CAF) and [[FIFA]] since 1986.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Sport in The Seychelles|url=https://www.topendsports.com/world/countries/seychelles.htm|website=topendsports.com|access-date=2020-05-29|archive-date=21 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220121112145/https://www.topendsports.com/world/countries/seychelles.htm|url-status=live}}</ref> The team's home stadium is the 10,000-capacity [[Stade Linité]] located in [[Roche Caiman]] in the outskirts of [[Victoria, Seychelles|Victoria]], the capital of Seychelles.<ref name=":0" />

== History ==

Football was introduced to Seychelles in the 1930s. Its first official competition, the Challenge Cup, was organized in 1936. Then in 1941, a championship between five teams was set up with matches of 60 minutes, played barefoot.<ref>{{cite web |title=History Of Seychelles Football & Federation |url=http://www.cerf-resort.com/seychellesfootball.html |website=Cerf Resort |access-date=25 September 2020 |archive-date=13 August 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160813214541/http://www.cerf-resort.com/seychellesfootball.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

In 1969, the president of the [[Seychelles Football Federation]] wanted to set up a national team. Engaged on a voluntary basis, [[Adrian Fisher (football coach)|Adrian Fisher]] arrived on the island in September to reorganize Seychelles football. He equipped all the players with shoes, introduced contemporary training techniques and extended the match time to 90 minutes. The newly-formed national team played their first matches at a friendly tournament in Kenya in April 1970. In Seychelles' first matches played on a real football pitch, they drew 2–2 against [[Feisal F.C.]] then lost 1–2 to Mwengi at [[Mombasa Municipal Stadium]]. When Fisher left Seychelles in 1973, the team had played six games against four clubs.

Seychelles played their first match against another national team on March 13, 1974, against [[Réunion national football team|Réunion]]. In this friendly match, the ''Pirates'' lost 0–2. Two years later, this time as an independent nation, the Seychelles faced Réunion again, losing 1–4. In September 1977, they played their first match against a FIFA-affiliated team, [[Mauritius national football team|Mauritius]], during a tournament played in Réunion, and lost 1–2. The team enjoyed its first victory the following year by beating Réunion 1–0 at home.

[[File:Seychelles v Ethiopia 5 September 2015.jpg|thumb|right|Seychelles v [[Ethiopia national football team|Ethiopia]] at [[Stade Linité]], 5 September 2015]]
In 1979, Seychelles played their first tournament, the [[Football at the 1979 Indian Ocean Island Games|1979 Indian Ocean Games]]. They lost their first game 3–0 to [[Réunion national football team|Réunion]], and beat [[Maldives national football team|Maldives]] 9–0 in their second. In the semi-final, they knocked out [[Mauritius national football team|Mauritius]] 4–2 on penalties after a 1–1 draw, but in the final they lost 2–1 to Réunion.

Seychelles were eliminated from the group stage at the [[Football at the 1985 Indian Ocean Island Games|1985 Indian Ocean Games]] but, on August 31, 1986, they played their first competitive match recognised by [[FIFA]] and [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]], following their affiliation with both organisations, a qualification match against Mauritius for the [[Football at the 1987 All-Africa Games|1987 All-Africa Games]], which they lost 1–2. They participated, for the first time, in the [[1988 African Cup of Nations]] qualifiers against Mauritius but lost 1–3 over the two legs.

At the [[Football at the 1990 Indian Ocean Island Games|1990 Indian Ocean Games]], the team suffered the heaviest defeat in its history against [[Madagascar national football team|Madagascar]] in the semi-final, losing 0–6. In the bronze medal match, they won against [[Comoros national football team|Comoros]] 3–1. Despite hosting the [[Football at the 1993 Indian Ocean Island Games|following games]], the team failed to repeat this performance, losing all four games and finishing last in the competition.

After the elimination from the preliminary phase of the [[1996 African Cup of Nations]] by Mauritius 2–1 over the two legs, [[Vojo Gardašević]] was hired to lead the team. Following that, the ''Pirates'' again finished third in the [[Football at the 1998 Indian Ocean Island Games|1998 Indian Ocean Games]] and, two years later, competed in the [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF first round|World Cup qualifiers]] for the first time. Playing against [[Namibia national football team|Namibia]] they drew 1–1 in [[Stade Linité]], thanks to a goal by [[Philip Zialor]] but lost the second leg 0–3. Seychelles were also eliminated in the preliminary round of the [[2000 African Cup of Nations qualification|2000 African Cup of Nations]] by [[Zimbabwe national football team|Zimbabwe]] 0–6 over the two matches. Seychelles fared better in the qualification for the [[2004 African Cup of Nations qualification|2004 African Cup of Nations]] as the team, led by [[Dominique Bathenay]] then [[Michael Nees]], finished third and recorded two prestigious home wins: beating [[Eritrea national football team|Eritrea]] 1–0 with a goal by veteran [[Roddy Victor]], and beating a [[Peter Ndlovu]]-led Zimbabwe team 2–1 with goals by strikers [[Alpha Baldé]] and [[Philip Zialor]]. Following that they achieved a bronze medal at the [[Football at the 2003 Indian Ocean Island Games|2003 Indian Ocean Games]], then were eliminated in the preliminary round of the [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF first round|2006 World Cup]] by [[Zambia national football team|Zambia]], 1–5 in the two games.

In [[2011 in Seychellois football|2011]], Seychelles hosted the [[Football at the 2011 Indian Ocean Island Games|2011 Indian Ocean Island Games]] and won the tournament for the first time, beating [[Mauritius national football team|Mauritius]] in the final on penalties.<ref>{{Cite web|last=FIFA.com|date=27 August 2020|title=Spectacular Seychelles' footballing passion|url=https://www.fifa.com/news/spectacular-seychelles-footballing-passion|access-date=2020-09-25|language=en-GB|archive-date=25 September 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200925194036/https://www.fifa.com/news/spectacular-seychelles-footballing-passion|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last=FIFA.com|date=1 September 2011|title=Seychelles making historic waves|url=https://www.fifa.com/fifa-world-ranking/news/seychelles-making-historic-waves-1502486|access-date=2020-09-25|language=en-GB}}{{dead link|date=October 2021|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref>

The rest of the 2010s saw little success for ''The Pirates'' with the high point achieved during [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations qualification]] where they managed a 2–0 win over [[Lesotho national football team|Lesotho]] and a 1–1 draw with [[Ethiopia national football team|Ethiopia]] to finish third in their four-team group.{{Citation needed|date=May 2020}}

== FIFA Goal programme ==
In 2006, a new technical centre in [[Mahé, Seychelles|Mahé]] was opened, with help from the FIFA Goal programme.<ref>{{Cite web|date=21 April 2009|title=Seychelles FIFA Goal Project|url=https://www.fifa.com/mm/goalproject/sey_eng.pdf|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180317231938/https://www.fifa.com/mm/goalproject/sey_eng.pdf|archive-date=17 March 2018|access-date=24 September 2020|website=FIFA.com}}</ref> The project had a total cost of approximately 750,000 USD. [[James Michel]], president of the Seychelles, was present at the inauguration. The technical centre houses the SFF headquarters, an auditorium, 20 bedrooms, two massage rooms, changing rooms and a restaurant. The centre is located next to the turf pitches that were also installed by the Goal programme in 2003.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Seychelles Football Federation & history|url=http://www.cerf-resort.com/seychellesfootball.html|access-date=2020-09-25|website=www.cerf-resort.com|archive-date=13 August 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160813214541/http://www.cerf-resort.com/seychellesfootball.html|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=16 February 2006|title=Goal Project 2-Seychelles' football house inaugurated|url=http://www.nation.sc/archive/212893/football-goal-project-2-seychelles-football-house-inaugurated|access-date=2020-09-25|website=[[Seychelles Nation]]|archive-date=21 November 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211121015009/https://www.nation.sc/archive/212893/football-goal-project-2-seychelles-football-house-inaugurated|url-status=live}}</ref>

==Results and fixtures==
{{main|Seychelles national football team results}}
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group F|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|date       = {{Start date|2025|9|3|df=y}}
|time       = {{UTZ|17:00|4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SEY}}
|score      = 0–4
|team2      = {{fb|GAB}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
*[[Denis Bouanga|Bouanga]] {{goal|4}}, {{goal|34}}, {{goal|38}}
*[[Yannis M'Bemba|M'Bemba]] {{goal|89}}
|stadium    = [[Côte d'Or National Sports Complex]]
|location   = [[Saint Pierre, Mauritius|Saint Pierre]], Mauritius
|attendance =
|referee    = Jean Ouattara ([[Burkinabé Football Federation|Burkina Faso]])
|report     = [https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020795 Report (FIFA)]<br>[https://www.cafonline.com/fifa-world-cup/match-centre?competition=340&season=112026&match=2407562 Report (CAF)]
|result     = L
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group F|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 9 September
|time       =
|team1      = {{fb-rt|KEN}}
|score      = 5–0
|team2      = {{fb|SEY}}
|goals1     =
*[[Ryan Ogam]] {{goal|7||38}}
*[[Collins Sichenje]] {{goal|35}}
*[[Michael Olunga]] {{goal|45+5|pen.|67}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Moi International Sports Centre]]
|location   = [[Nairobi]], Kenya
|attendance =
|referee    = Joseph Odey Ogabor ([[Nigeria Football Federation|Nigeria]])
|report     = [https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020799 Report (FIFA)]<br>[https://www.cafonline.com/fifa-world-cup/match-centre?competition=340&season=112026&match=2407565 Report (CAF)]
|result     = L
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group F|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 6 October
|time       = {{UTZ|17:00|4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SEY}}
|score      = 0–7
|team2      = {{fb|CIV}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
*[[Ibrahim Sangaré (Ivorian footballer)|Sangaré]] {{goal|7|pen.}}
*[[Emmanuel Agbadou|Agbadou]] {{goal|17}}
*[[Oumar Diakité|Diakité]] {{goal|32}}
*[[Evann Guessand|Guessand]] {{goal|39}}
*[[Yan Diomande|Diomande]] {{goal|55}}
*[[Simon Adingra|Adingra]] {{goal|67}}
*[[Franck Kessié|Kessié]] {{goal|90}}
|stadium    = [[Côte d'Or National Sports Complex]]
|location   = [[Saint Pierre, Mauritius|Saint Pierre]], Mauritius
|attendance =
|referee    = Jean Ishimwe ([[Rwanda Football Federation|Rwanda]])
|report     = 
|result     = L
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group F|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 14 October
|time       =
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SEY}}
|score      = 0–7
|team2      = {{fb|GAM}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Côte d'Or National Sports Complex]]
|location   = [[Saint Pierre, Mauritius|Saint Pierre]], Mauritius
|attendance =
|referee    =
|report     =
|result     = L
}}

===2026===
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification]]
|date       = 26 March 2026
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|2}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SEY}}
|score      = 0–0
|team2      = {{fb|LES}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = [[Bloemfontein]] South Africa
|stadium    = [[Free State Stadium|Toyota Stadium]]
|attendance =
|referee    = [[William Oloya]] ([[Federation of Uganda Football Associations|Uganda]])
|report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-26/seychelles-vs-lesotho/4048697
|result     = D
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification]]
|date       = 29 March 2026
|time       = {{UTZ|17:00|2}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|LES}}
|score      = 2–1
|team2      = {{fb|SEY}}
|goals1     =
*[[Lebohang Lesako|Lesako]] {{goal|50}}
*[[Sera Motebang|Motebang]] {{goal|82}}
|goals2     =
*[[Brandon Labrosse|Labrosse]] {{goal|45+2}}
|location   = [[Bloemfontein]], South Africa
|stadium    = [[Free State Stadium|Toyota Stadium]]
|attendance =
|referee    = Gerson Esono ([[Equatoguinean Football Federation|Equatorial Guinea]])
|report     = [https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-29/lesotho-vs-seychelles/4048698 Report]
|result     = L
}}

==Coaching staff==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Position
! Name
|-
| Head coach
|{{flagicon|SEY}} [[Ralph Jean-Louis]] (caretaker)
|-
| Assistant Coaches 
|{{flagicon|SEY}} Ernest Vivien<br>{{flagicon|SEY}} Lesley Guichard
|-
| Goalkeeping Coach 
|{{flagicon|SEY}} Frantz Boulle
|-
| Fitness Coach 
|{{flagicon|SEY}} James Fontaine
|-
| Match Analyst
|{{flagicon|SEY}} Alexis Lafleur
|-
| Doctors
|{{flagicon|SEY}} Dr. Daniel François<br>{{flagicon|SEY}} Dr. Justine Valmont
|-
| Physiotherapists 
|{{flagicon|SEY}} Michael Payet<br>{{flagicon|SEY}} Jules Hervé<br>{{flagicon|SEY}} Richard Lemoine<br>{{flagicon|SEY}} Thomas Hoareau
|-
| Team Coordinator 
|{{flagicon|SEY}} [[Gavin Jeanne]]
|-
| Technical Director 
|{{flagicon|SEY}} [[Vivian Bothe]]
|}

===Coaching history===
:Caretaker managers are listed in ''italics''.

{{Div col|colwidth=22em}}
*{{flagicon|ENG}} [[Adrian Fisher (football coach)|Adrian Fisher]] (1969–1973)
*{{flagicon|SEY|1977}} [[Ulrich Mathiot]] (1991)
*{{flagicon|GER}} [[Helmut Kosmehl]] (1992–1993)
*{{flagicon|FRY}} [[Vojo Gardašević]] (1997–2001)
*{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Dominique Bathenay]] (2002)
*{{flagicon|GER}} [[Michael Nees]] (2002–2004)
*{{flagicon|COD}} [[Raoul Shungu]] (2006–2008)
*{{flagicon|NED}} [[Jan Mak]] (2008)
*{{flagicon|SEY}} [[Ulrich Mathiot]] (2008)
*{{flagicon|SWE}} [[Richard Holmlund]] (2009)
*{{flagicon|NED}} [[Jan Mak]] (2010)
*{{flagicon|GER}} [[Michael Nees]] (2010)
*{{flagicon|ENG}} [[Andrew Amers-Morrison]] (2010)
*{{flagicon|SEY}} [[Ralph Jean-Louis]] (2010–2011)
*{{flagicon|SEY}} ''[[Gavin Jeanne]]'' (2012)
*{{flagicon|NED}} [[Jan Mak]] (2013–2014)
*{{flagicon|SEY}} [[Ulrich Mathiot]] (2014–2015)
*{{flagicon|MAD}} ''[[Bruno Saindini]]'' (2015)
*{{flagicon|SEY}} [[Ralph Jean-Louis]] (2015–2016)
*{{flagicon|SEY}} ''[[Joel de Commarmond]]'' (2017)
*{{flagicon|SEY}} ''[[Rodney Choisy]]'' (2017)
*{{flagicon|SEY}} [[Gavin Jeanne]] (2018–2019)
*{{flagicon|NED}} [[Jan Mak]] (2019)
*{{flagicon|SEY}} [[Ralph Jean-Louis]] (2020–2021)
*{{flagicon|FRA}} ''[[Osama Haroun]]'' (2021)
*{{flagicon|SEY}} [[Vivian Bothe]] (2021–2023)
*{{flagicon|SEY}} ''[[Ralph Jean-Louis]]'' (2023–)
{{div col end}}

== Players ==
=== Current squad ===
The following players were selected for the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group F]] matches against [[Gabon national football team|Gabon]] and [[Kenya national football team|Kenya]] on 3 and 9 September 2025.<ref>{{cite web |title=FIFA World Cup 2026 qualifiers are coming |url=https://www.facebook.com/photo?fbid=1336790435114124&set=pcb.1336790471780787&locale=en_GB |access-date=28 August 2025 |website=Facebook |publisher=Seychelles Football Federation |date=28 August 2025}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 25 March 2025, after the match against [[Burundi national football team|Burundi]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=1|pos=GK|name=[[Carlos Simeon]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|10|29|df=yes}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Anse Réunion FC|Anse Réunion]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=18|pos=GK|name=Romeo Padayachy|age={{Bda|1993|2|18|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Light Stars FC|Light Stars]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=22|pos=GK|name=Ian Ah-Kong|age={{Bda|1995|11|2|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=0|club=[[Anse Réunion FC|Anse Réunion]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=2|pos=DF|name=Justin Riaze|age={{birth date and age|2002|11|14|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Saint Louis Suns United FC|Saint Louis Suns United]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=3|pos=DF|name=[[Makhan Bristol-Cissoko]]|age={{Bda|2006|6|26|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[Foresters (Mont Fleuri)|Foresters Mont Fleuri]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=4|pos=DF|name=[[Alfie Walters]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|09|01|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Metropolitan Police F.C.|Metropolitan Police]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=5|pos=DF|name=Don Fanchette|age={{Bda|1997|12|3|df=y}}|caps=31|goals=0|club=[[La Passe FC|La Passe]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=12|pos=DF|name=[[Warren Mellie]]|age={{Bda|1994|10|1|df=y}}|caps=40|goals=3|club=[[Foresters (Mont Fleuri)|Foresters Mont Fleuri]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=19|pos=DF|name=Markus Payet|age={{Bda|2005|6|13|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[La Passe FC|La Passe]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=8|pos=MF|name=[[Lorenzo Hoareau]]|age={{Bda|2007|6|1|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=3|club=[[Anse Réunion FC|Anse Réunion]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=10|pos=MF|name=Dean Mothe|age={{Bda|2000|8|1|df=y}}|caps=25|goals=0|club=[[Saint Louis Suns United FC|Saint Louis Suns United]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=14|pos=MF|name=Anil Dijoux|age={{Bda|2006|7|20|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Anse Réunion FC|Anse Réunion]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=16|pos=MF|name=[[Josip Ravignia]]|age={{Bda|2001|1|19|df=y}}|caps=20|goals=0|club=[[St Michel United FC|St Michel United]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=17|pos=MF|name=[[Ryan Henriette]]|age={{Bda|2001|1|23|df=y}}|caps=22|goals=2|club=[[Anse Réunion FC|Anse Réunion]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=20|pos=MF|name=Imra Raheriniaina|age={{Bda|2004|11|19|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=0|club=[[Saint Louis Suns United FC|Saint Louis Suns United]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=21|pos=MF|name=Vince Fred|age={{Bda|2005|10|16|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Light Stars FC|Light Stars]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=23|pos=MF|name=Elie Sopha|age={{Bda|2004|11|3|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[St Michel United FC|St Michel United]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=6|pos=FW|name=Anelka Adela|age={{Bda|2006|11|28|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Bel Air FC|Bel Air]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=7|pos=FW|name=[[Brandon Labrosse]]|age={{Bda|1999|3|11|df=y}}|caps=32|goals=4|club=[[Foresters (Mont Fleuri)|Foresters Mont Fleuri]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=9|pos=FW|name=Jerone Joubert|age={{Bda|2003|11|28|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Light Stars FC|Light Stars]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=11|pos=FW|name=[[Tyrone Cadeau]]|age={{Bda|2004|6|14|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[Newcastle Town F.C.|Newcastle Town]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=13|pos=FW|name=[[Samuel Okolie]]|age={{bda|2008|3|22|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Cambridge United F.C.|Cambridge United]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=15|pos=FW|name=Torry Radegonde|age={{Bda|2003|1|11|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[La Passe FC|La Passe]]|clubnat=SEY}}
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|10 October 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Seychelles |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/165/Seychelles.html |website=National Football Teams |access-date=14 April 2023 |archive-date=8 July 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230708141639/https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/165/2023/Seychelles.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Seychelles.''

===Most appearances===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width="30"|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Name
!width="50"|Caps
!width="50"|Goals
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Benoit Marie]]'''
|64
|0
|2013–present
|-
|2
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Gervais Waye-Hive]]'''
|51
|6
|2012–present
|-
|3
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Colin Esther]]
|45
|2
|2008–2022
|-
|4
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Eric Nelson Sopha]]
|44
|0
|1996–2012
|-
|rowspan=2|5
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Jones Joubert]]
|43
|0
|2006–2019
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Warren Mellie]]'''
|43
|3
|2017–present
|-
|7
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Achille Henriette]]
|39
|6
|2006–2016
|-
|8
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Brandon Labrosse]]'''
|35
|4
|2017–present
|-
|rowspan=2|9
|style="text-align: left;"|{{interlanguage link|Henny Dufresne|it}}
|33
|1
|2002–2014
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Philip Zialor]]
|33
|14
|1998–2009
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===

{| class="wikitable sortable"  style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width="30"|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Name
!width="50"|Goals
!width="50"|Caps
!width="50"|Ratio
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Philip Zialor]]
|14
|33
|{{#expr:14/33 round 2}} 
|1998–2009
|-
|rowspan=3|2
|style="text-align: left;"|{{interlanguage link|Alpha Baldé|it}}
|6
|20
|{{#expr:6/20 round 2}} 
|1996–2011
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Achille Henriette]]
|6
|39
|{{#expr:6/39 round 2}} 
|2006–2016
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Gervais Waye-Hive]]'''
|6
|51
|{{#expr:6/51 round 2}} 
|2012–present
|-
|rowspan=3|5
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Perry Monnaie]]
|4
|17
|{{#expr:4/17 round 2}} 
|2018–2020
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|{{interlanguage link|Don Annacoura|it}}
|4
|27
|{{#expr:4/27 round 2}} 
|2000–2011
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Brandon Labrosse]]'''
|4
|35
|{{#expr:4/35 round 2}} 
|2019–present
|-
|rowspan=8|8
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Ralph Jean-Louis]]
|3
|10
|{{#expr:3/10 round 2}} 
|1993–2000
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Lorenzo Hoareau]]'''
|3
|12
|{{#expr:3/12 round 2}} 
|2021–present
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|{{interlanguage link|Wilnes Brutus|it}}
|3
|12
|{{#expr:3/12 round 2}} 
|2002–2011
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|{{interlanguage link|Leroy Coralie|es}}
|3
|17
|{{#expr:3/17 round 2}} 
|2016–2022
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Yelvanny Rose]]
|3
|18
|{{#expr:3/18 round 2}} 
|2002–2007
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Nelson Laurence]]
|3
|25
|{{#expr:3/25 round 2}} 
|2008–2018
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Godfrey Denis Armel]]
|3
|29
|{{#expr:3/29 round 2}} 
|1998–2008
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Warren Mellie]]'''
|3
|43
|{{#expr:3/43 round 2}}
|2017–present
|-
|}

== Competition records ==

=== FIFA World Cup ===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]]
!rowspan=39|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]]
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]
|colspan=8 |''Part of {{flag|United Kingdom}}''
|colspan=6 |''Part of {{flag|United Kingdom}}''
|-
| [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]] to [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]
|colspan=8 |''Not a FIFA member''
|colspan=6 |''Not a FIFA member''
|-
| [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]] to [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|colspan=8 |''Did not enter''
|colspan=6 |''Did not enter''
|-
| {{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=7|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|1
|1
|1
|4
|-
| {{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
|2
|0
|1
|1
|1
|5
|-
| {{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
|6
|0
|0
|6
|4
|17
|-
| {{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|7
|-
| {{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|10
|- 
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|10
|0
|0
|10
|2
|53
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total||–||{{Tooltip|0/9|Number of tournaments qualified for}}||–||–||–||–||–||–
!26
!0
!2
!24
!8
!99
|}

=== Africa Cup of Nations ===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[Africa Cup of Nations]] record
! rowspan="40" width="1%" |
!colspan=6|[[Africa Cup of Nations|Africa Cup of Nations Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1957 African Cup of Nations|1957]] to {{flagicon|Ethiopia|1975}} [[1976 African Cup of Nations|1976]]||colspan=8|''Part of {{flag|United Kingdom}}''
|colspan=6 |''Part of {{flag|United Kingdom}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1978 African Cup of Nations|1978]] to {{flagicon|Egypt}} [[1986 African Cup of Nations|1986]]||colspan=8|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|colspan=6|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[1988 African Cup of Nations|1988]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[1990 African Cup of Nations|1990]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2
|1
|0
|1
|1
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|Senegal}} [[1992 African Cup of Nations|1992]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1994 African Cup of Nations|1994]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[1996 African Cup of Nations|1996]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Burkina Faso}} [[1998 African Cup of Nations|1998]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|1
|1
|1
|2
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} {{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[2000 Africa Cup of Nations|2000]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Mali}} [[2002 Africa Cup of Nations|2002]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia}} [[2004 Africa Cup of Nations|2004]]||colspan=8 rowspan=4|''Did not qualify''
|6
|2
|0
|4
|4
|10
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2006 Africa Cup of Nations|2006]]
|2
|0
|1
|1
|1
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[2008 Africa Cup of Nations|2008]]
|6
|1
|1
|4
|3
|14
|-
|{{flagicon|Angola}} [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations|2010]]
|6
|0
|0
|6
|4
|17
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} {{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2012 Africa Cup of Nations|2012]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|7
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} [[2015 Africa Cup of Nations|2015]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]||colspan=8 rowspan=4|''Did not qualify''
|6
|1
|1
|4
|5
|11
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]
|6
|0
|1
|5
|3
|25
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]]
|2
|0
|0
|2
|1
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations|2023]]
|2
|0
|1
|1
|1
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]]
|colspan=8 |''Excluded''
|colspan=6 |''Excluded''
|-
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations|2027]]
| colspan="8" |''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|1
|1
|1
|2
|-
|{{flagicon|}} [[2029 Africa Cup of Nations|2029]]
| colspan="8" |''To be determined''
| colspan="6" |''To be determined''
|-
!Total||–||0/35||–||–||–||–||–||–||42||5||6||31||24||100
|}

===Indian Ocean Island Games===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=10|[[Football at the Indian Ocean Island Games|Indian Ocean Island Games]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Abbr|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Abbr|W|Won}}
!{{Abbr|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Abbr|L|Lost}}
!{{Abbr|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Abbr|GA|Goals against}}
|- style="background:silver;"
|{{flagicon|Réunion}} [[Football at the 1979 Indian Ocean Island Games|1979]]
|'''Runners-up'''
|'''2nd'''
|'''4'''
|'''1'''
|'''1'''
|'''2'''
|'''11'''
|'''6'''
|-
|{{flagicon|Mauritius}} [[Football at the 1985 Indian Ocean Island Games|1985]]
|Group stage
|6th
|2
|0
|0
|2
|1
|4
|- style="background:#c96;"
|{{flagicon|Madagascar}} [[Football at the 1990 Indian Ocean Island Games|1990]]
|'''Third place'''
|'''3rd'''
|'''3'''
|'''1'''
|'''0'''
|'''2'''
|'''3'''
|'''9'''
|- style="background:#9acdff;"
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|Seychelles|1977}} [[Football at the 1993 Indian Ocean Island Games|1993]]
|'''Fourth place'''
|'''4th'''
|'''4'''
|'''0'''
|'''0'''
|'''4'''
|'''3'''
|'''12'''
|- style="background:#c96;"
|{{flagicon|Réunion}} [[Football at the 1998 Indian Ocean Island Games|1998]]
|'''Third place'''
|'''3rd'''
|'''4'''
|'''2'''
|'''0'''
|'''2'''
|'''9'''
|'''10'''
|- style="background:#c96;"
|{{flagicon|Mauritius}} [[Football at the 2003 Indian Ocean Island Games|2003]]
|'''Third place'''
|'''3rd'''
|'''4'''
|'''1'''
|'''2'''
|'''1'''
|'''3'''
|'''6'''
|-
|{{flagicon|Madagascar}} [[Football at the 2007 Indian Ocean Island Games|2007]]
|Group stage
|5th
|2
|1
|0
|1
|2
|4
|- style="background:gold;"
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|Seychelles}} [[Football at the 2011 Indian Ocean Island Games|2011]]
|'''Champions'''
|'''1st'''
|'''5'''
|'''3'''
|'''2'''
|'''0'''
|'''10'''
|'''4'''
|-
|{{flagicon|Réunion}} [[Football at the 2015 Indian Ocean Island Games|2015]]
|Group stage
|5th
|3
|1
|0
|2
|4
|3
|- style="background:#9acdff;"
|{{flagicon|Mauritius}} [[Football at the 2019 Indian Ocean Island Games – Men's tournament|2019]]
|'''Fourth place'''
|'''4th'''
|'''4'''
|'''0'''
|'''2'''
|'''2'''
|'''2'''
|'''8'''
|-
|{{flagicon|Madagascar}} [[Football at the 2023 Indian Ocean Island Games – Men's tournament|2023]]
|Group Stage
|6th
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|2
|-
!Total
!1 Title
!11/11
!36
!10
!7
!20
!48
!68
|}

===CECAFA Cup===
{{Main|CECAFA Cup}}

===COSAFA Cup===
{{main|COSAFA Cup}}
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center; width:75%;"
|-
!rowspan="2"|Year
!colspan="8"|[[COSAFA Cup]]
|-
!Round||Result||M||W||D||L||GF||GA
|-
|[[2000 COSAFA Cup|2000]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Excluded''
|-
|[[2001 COSAFA Cup|2001]]
|-
|[[2002 COSAFA Cup|2002]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not enter''
|-
|[[2003 COSAFA Cup|2003]]
|-
|[[2004 COSAFA Cup|2004]]
|-
|[[2005 COSAFA Cup|2005]]
|Round 1
|11th
|1
|0
|0
|1
|0
|3
|-
|[[2006 COSAFA Cup|2006]]
|Round 1
|7th
|2
|0
|1
|1
|1
|3
|-
|[[2007 COSAFA Cup|2007]]
|Round 1
|13th
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|7
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2008 COSAFA Cup|2008]]
|Group stage
|11th
|3
|1
|1
|1
|8
|2
|-
|{{flagicon|Zimbabwe}} [[2009 COSAFA Cup|2009]]
|Group stage
|13th
|3
|0
|0
|3
|2
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|Zambia}} [[2013 COSAFA Cup|2013]]
|Group stage
|13th
|2
|0
|0
|2
|2
|8
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2015 COSAFA Cup|2015]]
|Group stage
|13th
|3
|0
|1
|2
|0
|2
|-
|{{flagicon|Namibia}} [[2016 COSAFA Cup|2016]]
|Group stage
|14th
|3
|0
|0
|3
|0
|10
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2017 COSAFA Cup|2017]]
|Group stage
|14th
|3
|0
|0
|3
|1
|10
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2018 COSAFA Cup|2018]]
|Group stage
|12th
|3
|0
|2
|1
|2
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2019 COSAFA Cup|2019]]
|Group stage
|13th
|3
|0
|1
|2
|0
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2021 COSAFA Cup|2021]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2022 COSAFA Cup|2022]]
|Group Stage
|13th
|3
|0
|0
|3
|1
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2023 COSAFA Cup|2023]]
|Group Stage
|12th
|3
|0
|0
|3
|2
|9
|-
!Total
!Group stage
!11/19
!34
!1
!6
!27
!19
!75
|}

== Head-to-head record == 
{{Updated|31 March 2026}}<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.eloratings.net/Seychelles|title=World Football Elo Ratings: Seychelles|publisher=Eloratings.net|date=4 May 2023|access-date=4 May 2023|archive-date=21 September 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220921181356/https://www.eloratings.net/Seychelles|url-status=live}}</ref>

===FIFA members===
{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center"
|-
!width=220|Opponent<br />
!width=100|Pld<br />
!width=70|W<br />
!width=70|D<br />
!width=70|L<br />
!width=100|GF<br />
!width=100|GA<br />
!width=120|GD<br />
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|ALG}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 6 || –6
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|ANG}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 2 || 6 || –4
|- bgcolor="#FFFACD"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|BAN}} || 3 || 1 || 1 || 1 || 2 || 2 || 0
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|BOT}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 3 || –3
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|BUR}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 3 || 7 || –4
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|BDI}} || 7 || 0 || 0 || 7 || 3 || 22 || –19
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|COM}} || 9 || 3 || 2 || 4 || 12 || 16 || –4
|- bgcolor="#FFFACD"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|CGO}} || 1 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 1 || 1 || 0
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|DRC}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 7 || –7
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|ERI}} || 3 || 1 || 0 || 2 || 1 || 2 || –1
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|SWZ}} || 4 || 0 || 1 || 3 || 1 || 7 || –6
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|ETH}} || 2 || 0 || 1 || 1 || 2 || 3 || –1
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|GAB}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 7 || –7
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|GAM}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 1 || 12 || –11
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|CIV}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 16 || –16
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|KEN}} || 7 || 0 || 1 || 6 || 2 || 26 || –24
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|LES}} || 8 || 1 || 3 || 4 || 7 || 10 || –3
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|LBY}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 2 || 13 || –11
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|MAD}} || 13 || 0 || 4 || 9 || 6 || 28 || –22
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|MWI}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 5 || –5
|- bgcolor="#CCFFCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|MDV}} || 8 || 4 || 1 || 3 || 21 || 10 || +11
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|MLI}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 5 || –5
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|MRI}} || 31 || 6 || 8 || 17 || 32 || 51 || –19
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|MOZ}} || 4 || 0 || 1 || 3 || 2 || 6 || –4
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|NAM}} || 7 || 0 || 3 || 4 || 5 || 15 || –10
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|NGA}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 1 || 6 || –5
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|PLE}} || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 1 || –1
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|RWA}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 10 || –10
|- bgcolor="#FFFACD"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|SMR}} || 1 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 0 || 0 || 0
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|SLE}} || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 2 || –2
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|RSA}} || 3 || 0 || 1 || 2 || 0 || 9 || –9
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|SSD}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 1 || 3 || –2
|- bgcolor="#CCFFCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|SRI}} || 4 || 2 || 1 || 1 || 8 || 5 || +3
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|SDN}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 5 || –5
|- bgcolor="#CCFFCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|TAN}} || 2 || 1 || 1 || 0 || 3 || 2 || +1
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|TUN}} || 4 || 0 || 0 || 4 || 0 || 14 || –14
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|UGA}} || 3 || 0 || 0 || 3 || 2 || 8 || –6
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|ZAM}} || 4 || 0 || 1 || 3 || 3 || 11 || –8
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|ZIM}} || 7 || 1 || 0 || 6 || 3 || 22 || –19
|- class="sortbottom"
! Total || 168 || 20 || 32 || 115 || 126 || 384 || –258
|}

===Non FIFA members===
{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center"
|-
!width=220|Opponent<br />
!width=100|Pld<br />
!width=70|W<br />
!width=70|D<br />
!width=70|L<br />
!width=100|GF<br />
!width=100|GA<br />
!width=120|GD<br />
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|MYT|local}} || 3 || 1 || 0 || 2 || 3 || 5 || –2
|- bgcolor="#FFCCCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|REU}} || 16 || 2 || 1 || 13 || 9 || 33 || –24
|- class="sortbottom"
! Total || 19 || 3 || 1 || 15 || 12 || 38 || –26
|}

==Honours==
===Regional===
* '''[[Football at the Indian Ocean Island Games|Indian Ocean Island Games]]'''
** {{Gold1}} '''Gold medal (1):''' [[Football at the 2011 Indian Ocean Island Games|2011]]
** {{Silver2}} Silver medal (1): [[Football at the 1979 Indian Ocean Island Games|1979]]
** {{Bronze3}} Bronze medal (3): [[Football at the 1990 Indian Ocean Island Games|1990]], [[Football at the 1998 Indian Ocean Island Games|1998]], [[Football at the 2003 Indian Ocean Island Games|2003]]

===Friendly===
* '''[[2021 Four Nations Football Tournament|Four Nations Football Tournament]] (1)''': 2021

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== External links ==
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/SEY Seychelles] at [[FIFA]]
* [https://www.cafonline.com/member-associations/sey/ Seychelles] at [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]]

{{Seychelles national football team managers}}
{{Football in Seychelles}}
{{COSAFA Football}}
{{CAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of Seychelles}}

[[Category:Seychelles national football team| ]]
[[Category:African national association football teams]]
[[Category:National sports teams established in 1974]]
